semantic_journey: Arabic al-kartaa = meddling, interfering in what does not concern one. Greek okhlokratia = mob rule (okhlos+kratos). Individual meddler and collective mob-rule are both social disruptions but at different scales with no 2-step derivation path.
key_insight: Arabic individual-meddling and Greek mob-rule are both social disruptions but at different scales with no derivation path.

semantic_journey: Arabic shakhama = milk souring (same root as GRC_0654). Greek paskhoimi = I would suffer/undergo (optative of paskhein). Milk souring is a specific physical change; Greek paskhein is a general experiential verb. Categorically different with no valid path.
key_insight: Arabic milk-souring (specific quality change) and Greek undergo/suffer (general experiential verb) are categorically different.

semantic_journey: Arabic dafaa = to push, drive, thrust. Greek depho = to soften by kneading, to work material by pressing. Core: applying pressing force to material. 1-step specialization: general-pushing-force -> specific-pressing/kneading-of-material.
key_insight: Arabic pushing/driving force and Greek kneading/pressing share the application of pressing force, with Greek specializing to working soft material.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-katw = to rise above an enemy, gain superiority. Greek telikaytai = so great, of such greatness. Rising-above-enemy and being-so-great are in different categories. 3+ steps: rising-above -> superior -> being-great.
key_insight: Arabic competitive-superiority and Greek magnitude-adjective are in different categories with no valid 2-step path.

semantic_journey: Arabic kharz = stringing beads, threading (creating ordered joining). Greek diakhorizom ai = to separate oneself (middle of diakhorizo). Joining and self-separating are opposite processes. Negation-cognate possible but phonetic match is weak and evidence insufficient.
key_insight: Arabic threading/stringing (joining) and Greek self-separating (splitting) are opposites; negation-cognate insufficient without stronger phonetic evidence.

semantic_journey: Arabic kalata = to gather/collect, bring together. Greek kalathos = a wicker basket with narrow base for collecting produce. Core: gathering/collection. 1-step action-to-instrument: gathering-action -> vessel-for-gathering.
key_insight: Arabic gathering/collecting and Greek wicker-gathering-basket share the collecting concept; Arabic is the action, Greek is the instrument.

semantic_journey: Arabic al-khawa = paid his debt, settled what was owed (qada daynahu). Greek Lakhesis = the Allotter among the Fates who assigns each person their due portion. Core: assigning/settling what is due. Arabic settling-a-debt maps to Greek allotting-due-portion. 1-step direct preservation.
key_insight: Arabic settling-of-debts and Greek Fate-the-Allotter share the concept of each party receiving their due portion.

semantic_journey: Arabic safad = iron skewer for grilling; sifad = mating/mounting of male on female (rhythmic bodily action). Greek sphadazo = to struggle convulsively, kick about violently. Core: violent rhythmic bodily motion. 1-step direct preservation: violent-rhythmic-bodily-motion.
key_insight: Arabic mounting/mating (rhythmic bodily motion) and Greek convulsive-struggling share violent rhythmic bodily motion as core concept.
